_doc: BWM Client Locks — machine-checkable contract for asap-pest-wildlife. Each assertion is a perceptual failure mode the team committed to never recur. Loaded by Brain's tools/bwm-client-locks-qa.py during PATCH and FULL BUILD. Drift from these locks = build failure. Source incidents: John's 2026-05-08 website-review email + Nehemiah's 2026-05-11 meeting (transcript at ~/Downloads/nehemiah-2026-05-11-transcript.md) + John's 2026-05-15 v8.1 review (over-removed ampersand + phone-tooth overlap).

required_strings.rationale: Sealing entry points is core to wildlife process — was replaced by 'Empathy' in v6, restored 2026-05-12 per client review.

required_assets.rationale: Item #5: All inner pages have the orange-tooth CTA banner. About was missing it pre-2026-05-12.
Item #9: Contact-form section uses the canonical monster, not the thin-mouth-line SVG.
Item #9: Contact-form section uses the canonical monster, not the thin-mouth-line SVG.
Homepage CTA banner shows the orange-tooth element.

John 2026-05-15 (correcting v8.1 over-removal): reference removeasap.com keeps ONE decorative '&' on the 'More' tab. We removed both in v8.1; restored the after-'More' instance in v8.2 to match reference. The Squirrels/Bees instance stays removed (reference doesn't have it).

forbidden_assets.rationale: Item #9: This is the deprecated thin-mouth-line monster. Never use in contact sections.

required_class_on_h1.rationale: Item #1: Inner-page H1s use the hollowed-out style matching homepage Peace-of-Mind treatment.

min_pattern_count.rationale: Item #4: Homepage partner-logo grid has 9 logos in a normalized grid (was unaligned flex-wrap before 2026-05-12).
Item #8: Commercial page partner-logo grid (Walmart alignment was the trigger).
